# CONTRACT.md — interfaces every component builds against

This file is the single source of truth for cross-component interfaces: file
ownership, JSON schemas, HTML class vocabulary, shared markup, design tokens,
and hard constraints. If code and this contract disagree, the contract wins;
if the contract must change, change it here first.

Repo root: `/home/rssndr/projects/denied.systems`

## 1. Directory layout & file ownership

```
build.sh                    # A — orchestrates the whole build
template.html               # A — pandoc template for topic pages
filters/wikilinks.lua       # A — [[wikilink]] resolution + edge recording
tools/build_index.py        # A — emits graph.json, search-index.json, manifest.json, wiki.html
content/                    # A — markdown sources (+ content/_template.md)
.github/workflows/build.yml # A — optional CI
index.html                  # B — hand-authored landing page (NOT generated)
style.css                   # B — the single stylesheet for the whole site
graph.html                  # C — static shell for the graph view
js/vendor/d3.js             # C — vendored d3 module bundle (see §8)
js/vendor/D3-LICENSE        # C — ISC license text
js/search.js                # C — shared search module
js/app.js                   # C — topic-page sidebar enhancement
js/nav.js                   # C — mobile hamburger + off-canvas drawer
js/assistant.js             # C — disabled stub (see §9)
assistant/README.md         # C — where the future RAG panel plugs in
--- generated by ./build.sh (never hand-edited, committed to git) ---
topics/<slug>.html          # one per content/*.md
wiki.html                   # generated listing page (by tools/build_index.py)
graph.json
search-index.json
manifest.json
--- scratch (gitignored) ---
.build/                     # intermediate files, e.g. edges.tsv
```

A = pipeline agent, B = design agent, C = client-JS agent. Touch only your
own files. `README.md` is written by the orchestrator at the end.

## 2. Content model

- One page = one `content/<slug>.md`. Slug: lowercase, hyphens, no spaces.
- Frontmatter (YAML, exactly this shape — keep parsing simple):

```yaml
---
title: "Human-readable title"
summary: "One sentence used for meta description, previews, search, wiki index."
tags: [navigation, sensor-fusion]
updated: 2026-07-07
---
```

- `title` and `summary` are plain text — no raw HTML, no `<` character, and
  never the sequence `</`. Rationale: pandoc interpolates them unescaped into
  `<title>`, the `meta description`/`og:`/`twitter:` `content` attributes and
  the `h1.page-title` (frontmatter is parsed as markdown, so inline HTML
  passes through verbatim), while `tools/build_index.py` entity-escapes the
  same strings for `wiki.html` — markup would render live on the topic page
  and inert on the wiki, and a literal `</title>` would break the whole
  `<head>`. `&`, `>` and unicode are fine (both emitters handle them). Also
  note pandoc smart-typography applies to topic pages only: straight quotes
  in a title render curly there but straight on `wiki.html` — type the exact
  punctuation you want (e.g. `’` `“` `”`) if the two views must match.
- Body must NOT start with an `# h1` — the template renders the title.
  Sections start at `##`.
- Wikilinks: `[[target-slug]]` or `[[target-slug|display text]]`. Target is the
  destination file name without `.md`. Parsed by pandoc's
  `wikilinks_title_after_pipe` extension; the Lua filter rewrites and records
  them. Broken targets become `<span class="wikilink broken">` + a stderr
  warning; the build does not fail.

## 3. Build pipeline contract

- `build.sh` (bash, `set -euo pipefail`) always rebuilds everything —
  idempotent: two consecutive runs produce byte-identical output.
- `SITE_URL` env var, default `https://denied.systems`, no trailing slash.
- Per page, pandoc is invoked with:
  `--from markdown+wikilinks_title_after_pipe --standalone --template template.html
   --lua-filter filters/wikilinks.lua --mathml
   --metadata pageurl="$SITE_URL/topics/<slug>.html"`
  and WITHOUT `--no-highlight` (built-in skylighting highlighting stays on).
- Environment passed to the Lua filter: `PAGE_SLUG` (source slug),
  `EDGES_FILE` (`.build/edges.tsv`, truncated at build start),
  `CONTENT_DIR` (`content`).
- Edge record format: one `source<TAB>target` line per resolved wikilink.
  Self-links are not recorded. Duplicates are fine — the indexer dedupes.
- After all pages: `python3 tools/build_index.py --content content
  --edges .build/edges.tsv --site-url "$SITE_URL" --out .`
  (Python 3, stdlib only. Must NOT import yaml/pip packages.)
- `content/_template.md` is skipped by the build.
- Nothing in the build may touch the network. No Node anywhere in the build.

## 4. JSON schemas (emitted by tools/build_index.py)

`graph.json`:
```json
{ "nodes": [{ "id": "slug", "title": "…", "url": "topics/slug.html",
              "tags": ["…"], "degree": 0 }],
  "edges": [{ "source": "slug-a", "target": "slug-b" }] }
```
- `degree` = backlink count (in-degree over deduped edges).
- Edges deduped on (source, target). Both endpoints must exist as nodes.
- All `url` values are site-root-relative (no leading slash).

`search-index.json` (top-level array):
```json
[{ "id": "slug", "title": "…", "summary": "…", "url": "topics/slug.html",
   "tags": ["…"], "updated": "2026-07-07", "text": "plain text of the page" }]
```
- `text`: markdown stripped to plain text (frontmatter removed, wikilinks →
  their display text, code kept as text, math kept as-is minus `$` fences,
  markdown syntax characters removed, whitespace collapsed).

`manifest.json` (for a future retrieval layer — do not consume it in v1 JS):
```json
{ "schema": 1, "site": "https://denied.systems",
  "pages": [{ "id", "title", "summary", "tags", "updated", "url", "words" }] }
```

## 5. Shared markup (exact structures — CSS is written against these)

Every page includes in `<head>`: `charset`, `viewport`, `<title>… — denied.systems</title>`,
the stylesheet (`style.css`, path-relative), and this favicon line verbatim:

```html
<link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 16 16'%3E%3Crect width='16' height='16' fill='%23070706'/%3E%3Ccircle cx='8' cy='8' r='2.2' fill='%23d4643a'/%3E%3Ccircle cx='8' cy='8' r='5.5' fill='none' stroke='%23d4643a' stroke-opacity='.45' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='2 3'/%3E%3C/svg%3E">
```

(The `xmlns` URL is a namespace identifier inside a data: URI, not a network
request — whitelisted for the zero-external-requests check.)

Shared header, all pages (hrefs adjusted for depth — topic pages prefix `../`):

```html
<header class="site-head">
  <a class="brand" href="index.html">denied<span class="brand-tld">.systems</span></a>
  <nav class="site-nav">
    <a href="index.html">home</a>
    <a href="wiki.html">wiki</a>
    <a href="graph.html">graph</a>
  </nav>
</header>
```

Active-link convention: the `.site-nav` link for the current view carries
`class="active"` — the home link on `index.html`, the wiki link on
`wiki.html`, the graph link on `graph.html`. Topic pages mark nothing. CSS
styles it as `.site-nav a.active { color: var(--accent) }` (hover on the
other links is unaffected). There is no separate view toggle.

`.site-head` renders at a constant height on every page (CSS `min-height`
≈3.3rem with centered items); the search widget must stay compact enough
not to grow the row. Wrapped rows on narrow screens may grow — acceptable.

The header is identical on every page — the landing (`index.html`),
`wiki.html`, `graph.html`, **and topic pages** all carry the same
`.site-nav` and the search widget after it:

```html
<div class="search">
  <input id="search-in" type="search" placeholder="search…" autocomplete="off">
  <div id="search-out" class="search-out" hidden></div>
</div>
```

Topic page body (`template.html` renders this; `$…$` are pandoc variables).
Its header carries the search widget too (with `../`-relative asset paths);
search.js is loaded and boots in navigate mode:

```html
<body class="page-topic">
  <header class="site-head">…(nav ../index.html etc., then the .search widget)…</header>
  <div class="layout">
    <nav class="sidebar" id="sidebar" aria-label="all pages"></nav>
    <article class="prose">
      <p class="kicker">field guide</p>
      <h1 class="page-title">$title$</h1>
      <p class="lede">$summary$</p>
      <p class="page-meta">
        <span class="updated">updated $updated$</span>
        <span class="tag" data-tag="$tags$">$tags$</span>  <!-- $for(tags)$ loop -->
      </p>
      $body$
    </article>
  </div>
  <footer class="page-foot">
    <span>denied.systems — a field guide to autonomy in contested airspace</span>
    <a href="../index.html">← index</a>
  </footer>
  <script src="../js/app.js"></script>
  <script src="../js/search.js"></script>
  <script>SiteSearch.boot('wiki')</script>
  <script src="../js/nav.js"></script>
</body>
```

Generated `wiki.html` main content (emitted by `tools/build_index.py`; body
class `page-wiki`; groups = primary tag = first tag, sections sorted
alphabetically, pages by title). The listing is wrapped in the same
`.layout` as topic pages, with the js/app.js sidebar mount before it
(`<div class="layout"><nav class="sidebar" id="sidebar" …></nav> … </div>`),
and the page loads `js/app.js` before the search scripts:

```html
<main class="wiki-index">
  <p class="kicker">index of all pages</p>
  <section class="tag-group" data-tag="navigation">
    <h2 class="tag-head">navigation</h2>
    <ul class="page-list">
      <li><a href="topics/slug.html">
        <span class="pl-title">Title</span>
        <span class="pl-summary">Summary…</span>
        <span class="pl-meta">updated 2026-07-07</span>
      </a></li>
    </ul>
  </section>
</main>
```

`wiki.html` and `graph.html` both end with the hidden assistant stub (§9) and:

```html
<script src="js/search.js"></script>
<script>SiteSearch.boot('wiki')</script>   <!-- or 'graph' -->
```

`graph.html` main content (before the scripts, which also include
`js/vendor/d3.js` then `js/graph.js` before `SiteSearch.boot('graph')`):

```html
<main class="graph-wrap">
  <svg id="graph" role="img" aria-label="knowledge graph"></svg>
  <div class="graph-legend" id="graph-legend"></div>
  <p class="graph-hint">scroll to zoom · drag to pan · click a node to open its page</p>
</main>
```

`index.html` (landing): body class `page-landing`, shared header, then a
narrative `<main class="landing">` — hero (`h1.site-title`, `p.site-sub`),
short "map of the terrain" sections with prose links to `topics/*.html`,
`wiki.html`, `graph.html`. Placeholder prose, clearly replaceable.

## 6. Client JS APIs

All plain scripts / IIFEs — no ES modules, no bundler, works over `file://`
except where `fetch` is inherently needed (graph, search, sidebar), which
degrade silently to static content when `fetch` fails.

`js/search.js` exposes exactly:
```js
window.SiteSearch = {
  boot(mode),        // 'wiki' | 'graph': fetches search-index.json, wires
                     // #search-in / #search-out with keyboard (↑↓ Enter Esc)
                     // and click selection. The keyboard-selected result row
                     // carries class `sel` (styled as `.search-out > .sel`).
  query(q)           // → ranked [{id,title,summary,url,tags}] (for reuse/tests)
}
```
- Loaded on the landing, `wiki.html`, `graph.html`, and topic pages. It is
  depth-aware: on `body.page-topic` it prefixes the index fetch and result
  navigation with `../` (index and result urls are site-root-relative), so
  search works from inside `topics/`.
- Selection in mode 'wiki': navigate to `result.url` (prefixed on topics).
- Selection in mode 'graph': call `window.GraphView.focusOnNode(result.id)`
  (fall back to navigation if `GraphView` is absent).
- Ranking: custom index built at boot — weights title > tags > summary > text;
  last query term matches by prefix. No external libs.

`js/graph.js` exposes exactly:
```js
window.GraphView = { focusOnNode(id) }  // animated zoom/center on node by slug
```
Behavior (fixed, no UI sliders): zoom/pan (d3-zoom, scale extent [0.1, 8]),
node drag (d3-drag) that keeps the simulation hot (alphaTarget 0.3 while
dragging, node position released on end) so neighbors follow elastically,
click navigates to `node.url`, node radius `4 + 3*sqrt(degree)`, color =
primary tag via the tag palette (§7, assigned to tags sorted by frequency
then name; wraps if >6). Force model (elastic, Obsidian-like, ported from
the owner's hive-mind prototype): link distance ~80 at d3's DEFAULT link
strength (no explicit strength), charge ~-250 with no distanceMax, a very
weak `d3.forceCenter(0,0)` (strength ~0.001), collide radius = node radius
+ 5; simulation coordinates centered on (0,0) with the initial view set
programmatically to `translate(w/2,h/2).scale(0.8)` and recentered on
resize. Hovering a node fades non-neighbors and non-incident links
(adjacency built with the `e.source.id || e.source` pattern on BOTH
endpoints — never a bare `|| target`); hovering a link fades everything but
its two endpoints; hover is inert while a node is focused and clears on svg
mouseleave. `focusOnNode(id)` marks the node with an accent ring, fades
non-neighbors and all links, then chains zoom transitions translateTo
(~1000ms) and scaleTo 2.8 (~800ms); a subsequent MANUAL zoom/pan (detected
via `event.sourceEvent` + a userHasInteracted flag) or a node drag clears
the focus state; unknown ids return false so search.js falls back to
navigation. Label level-of-detail keyed to zoom `k`: k > 0.7 all labels;
0.45 < k ≤ 0.7 only top-quartile-degree labels (min 1); k ≤ 0.45 none
(smooth opacity transitions). Label LOD stays live during hover and focus
fades: highlighted labels (the focused/hovered node and its neighbors, or
a hovered link's endpoints) always follow the CURRENT tier, de-emphasised
labels sit at the faded opacity — crossing a tier boundary mid-fade must
neither un-fade de-emphasised labels nor freeze highlighted ones at a
stale tier. Legend of tag→color in `#graph-legend`. Force
params baked in one clearly-marked const block. On fetch/init failure:
`.graph-error` message in `.graph-wrap` and `window.GraphView` is removed
entirely (teardown) so search falls back to navigation.

`js/app.js` (topic pages and wiki.html): fetch `search-index.json` (prefix `../` on topic pages), render the sidebar
`#sidebar` as tag-grouped links (same grouping as wiki.html), mark the
current page `.current`, include a small client-side filter input
`input.sidebar-filter`. The `.sidebar` reserves its width in CSS while
empty (so the async fill does not shift the article on load); on fetch
failure or an empty index, app.js sets the `hidden` attribute to collapse
that reserved column.

`js/nav.js` (loaded on index.html, wiki.html, graph.html, and topic pages —
any page with a `.search` and/or `#sidebar`): builds the mobile navigation.
It injects a hamburger button as the first child of `.site-head` and an
off-canvas `.drawer` + `.nav-backdrop` at the end of `<body>`. At ≤900px
(matchMedia) it MOVES the page's existing `.search` and `#sidebar` nodes
into the drawer and back to their original parents above that width, so the
desktop layout is unchanged and no markup is duplicated. Toggling adds
`body.nav-open`; Escape, backdrop click, and activating a link or search
result inside the drawer all close it.

The landing (`index.html`) loads `js/search.js` (its search box) and
`js/nav.js`; selecting a search result navigates to the page (it boots in
the navigate mode, like the wiki). Search results across all views carry
site-root-relative urls, which resolve from `index.html`/`wiki.html`/
`graph.html` directly and from topic pages via the sidebar instead.

## 7. Design tokens (defined once in style.css as CSS custom properties)

```css
--bg: #070706;        /* page background — true warm black   */
--surface: #0d0c0a;   /* panels, code blocks                 */
--surface-2: #15120e; /* hover, inline code                  */
--line: #29241e;      /* hairline borders, warm              */
--text: #d6cfc4;      /* body text, warm gray                */
--dim: #948b7e;       /* secondary text                      */
--faint: #5f584e;     /* metadata, hints                     */
--accent: #d4643a;    /* burnt signal orange — links, brand  */
--accent-hi: #e58753; /* lighter orange — hovers, code kw    */
--accent-2: #8296ad;  /* cool steel counterpoint             */
/* tag palette, in assignment order: */
--tag-1: #d4643a;  --tag-2: #8296ad;  --tag-3: #96ad82;
--tag-4: #c8a565;  --tag-5: #a08cb0;  --tag-6: #948b7e;
--mono: ui-monospace, "DejaVu Sans Mono", "Liberation Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
--sans: system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif;
```

Identity: true warm black, burnt signal orange accent. **Monospace is the
default everywhere** — the whole site (chrome, the landing, the wiki index)
is mono; `body` sets `var(--mono)`. The one exception is **the note text
itself**: `.prose` (a topic page's article body — paragraphs, lists,
ledes, table cells) switches to the system `--sans` for legibility, while
headings, code, kicker, title and meta inside `.prose` stay mono (they set
it explicitly and win). So the mono/sans split is scoped to topic-page
reading prose only. The mono stack is the system monospace (no bundled
web font). Warm hairline borders, `//`-prefixed kickers, a faint blueprint grid behind page content
on the landing, wiki, and graph views (the `.site-head` paints solid
`--bg` over it, so the header reads all-black on every page),
dense-but-legible (mono body 15.5px/1.7; the sans note prose 16px/1.65,
measure ~42rem). `html` sets
`scrollbar-gutter: stable both-edges` so header margins stay symmetric.
Mobile (≤900px): the header collapses to a hamburger that opens an
off-canvas drawer holding the search + sidebar (see `js/nav.js`); the graph
remains usable and its desktop gesture hint is hidden on touch widths.

`style.css` must style: all §5 markup, pandoc prose output (h2–h4, tables,
blockquotes, figures), **MathML** (`math` display and inline), and pandoc's
skylighting output — `div.sourceCode > pre > code` with token spans
(`.kw .dt .dv .st .co .fu .op .va .cf .im .al .bn .sc .er` at minimum) — plus
`.wikilink`, `.wikilink.broken` (dashed underline, muted), and the search
dropdown. One file, no @import, no external fonts.

## 8. Vendored d3 (one-time, checked into the repo)

`js/vendor/d3.js` = concatenation, IN THIS ORDER, of the minified UMD dists
from jsDelivr (`https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/<pkg>@<ver>/dist/<pkg>.min.js`) —
these attach to a shared global `d3`:

```
d3-dispatch@3.0.1  d3-quadtree@3.0.1  d3-timer@3.0.1  d3-color@3.1.0
d3-interpolate@3.0.1  d3-selection@3.0.0  d3-ease@3.0.1  d3-transition@3.0.1
d3-drag@3.0.0  d3-zoom@3.0.0  d3-force@3.0.0
```

This is the dependency closure of the four modules the spec names
(selection, force, zoom, drag) plus transition support for animated
focus-on-node — still ~⅓ of the full d3 bundle. Strip any
`//# sourceMappingURL=…` lines. Prefix each module with a one-line banner
comment (`/* d3-dispatch@3.0.1 — ISC */`). Put the ISC license text in
`js/vendor/D3-LICENSE`. Downloading happens once, now, by the developer —
the served site makes zero network requests.

## 9. Assistant stub (deferred feature — v1 ships it disabled)

- `wiki.html` and `graph.html` include, just before the scripts:
  `<aside id="assistant" class="assistant-panel" hidden aria-label="assistant (disabled)"></aside>`
- `js/assistant.js` exists but is NOT included by any page in v1. It contains
  a documented, disabled module skeleton (`window.Assistant = { enabled: false, mount() {…} }`)
  showing that a future BYO-key RAG panel consumes `search-index.json` +
  `manifest.json`. No API calls, no key handling, no network code.
- `assistant/README.md` documents the plug-in point in a few paragraphs.

## 10. Hard constraints (checked by the test agents)

1. Zero external requests from every served page: no external `script src`,
   `link href`, `img src`, fonts, fetch/XHR URLs. Whitelisted: `xmlns`
   identifiers inside data: URIs and SVG, and `og:url`/`twitter:*` meta
   *content* values (metadata, not fetched). External `<a href>` navigation
   links are allowed in content prose only.
2. Build requires only: bash, pandoc (≥3.1, with Lua), python3 stdlib.
3. `./build.sh` run twice in a row → byte-identical outputs (idempotent).
4. Generated files carry a `<!-- generated by build.sh — do not hand-edit -->`
   comment (HTML) or are obviously machine-owned (JSON).
5. All inter-page paths relative — the site works from any subdirectory and
   from `file://` for plain reading.
6. Every referenced local asset exists (no 404s when served).
7. No tracking, no analytics, no service worker.
